---
title: "LinkedIn"
platform_slug: "linkedin"
sources:
  ugc:
    BR: data/regional/BR/linkedin/ugc.yml
    EU: data/regional/EU/linkedin/ugc.yml
    UK: data/regional/BR/linkedin/ugc.yml
  ads:
    BR: data/global/linkedin/ads.yml
    EU: data/regional/EU/linkedin/ads.yml
    UK: data/global/linkedin/ads.yml
---


## Transparency Assessment


```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
import sys
from pathlib import Path
project_root = Path.cwd()
while not (project_root / 'utils').exists() and project_root != project_root.parent:
    project_root = project_root.parent
if str(project_root) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(project_root))
from utils.responses_aggregator import generate_platform_question_sections
```

```{python}
#| label: platform-summary
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
from utils.platform_summary import generate_platform_summary
generate_platform_summary("linkedin")
```

LinkedIn was assessed under both the UGC and ADS frameworks across the BR, EU, and UK regions. On the UGC framework, the platform offers no publicly accessible API for extracting public user-generated content data in the BR and UK regions, where all special-criteria questions (UGC_SC1–SC4) were answered negatively, placing those assessments in the Not Available band. In the EU, LinkedIn operates a Beta Researcher Access Program established to comply with Article 40 of the Digital Services Act, which nominally provides `researchers_only` access under UGC_SC1 and UGC_SC3; however, a research access request submitted during the assessment period received no response, making empirical verification of any API functionality impossible, and UGC_SC2 and UGC_SC4 remain negative. On the ADS framework, the `LinkedIn Ad Library API` scores `full` on AD_SC1 (weight 0.50) in both the global and EU regions, representing the most significant positive finding: all ad types are queryable and data is retained for up to one year after an ad's last impression. In the EU region only, AD_SC3 also scores `full` because active and inactive ad run periods are verifiable; outside the EU this item cannot be assessed. AD_SC2 (GUI-based structured data export, weight 0.30) is negative across all regions, as the `LinkedIn Ad Library` GUI provides no data export mechanism. Engagement data, advertiser verification status, AI-content labelling, and ad spending information are absent across all regions. The ADS framework falls in the Deficient to Restricted band globally, driven by a strong API availability score offset by pervasive gaps in completeness and compliance criteria; the UGC framework is effectively Not Available outside the EU and remains unverifiable within it, which substantially limits independent research on platform content dynamics.

## Detailed Assessment


## User-Generated Content

```{python}
#| label: ugc-questions
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_platform_question_sections(
    platform="linkedin",
    question_type="ugc",
    year="2025",
    heading_level=3,
)
```

## Advertising

```{python}
#| label: ads-questions
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_platform_question_sections(
    platform="linkedin",
    question_type="ads",
    year="2025",
    heading_level=3,
)
```

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
